title: dodonaea viscosa f. spatulata
intro: Dodonaea viscosa f. spatulata, also known as the 'sticky hop bush', is a species of flowering plant native to Australia and New Caledonia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Dodonaea viscosa f. spatulata include infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
